Block.Para: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act (telephone, fax and Internet service to campaign offices)

This bill was introduced during the First Session of the 39th Parliament. Pursuant to the Standing Orders of the House of Commons, it is deemed to have been considered and approved at all stages completed at the time of prorogation of the First Session. The number of the bill remains unchanged.

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
The purpose of this enactment is to ensure that telephone, fax or Internet service is provided in a timely manner to the campaign offices of candidates in federal elections.

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: Canada Elections (telephone, fax and Internet service to campaign offices)

Block.Para: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act (telephone, fax and Internet service to campaign offices)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. The Canada Elections Act is amended by adding the following after section 348:

Para.change: 348.1 Where the official agent of a candidate requests telephone, fax or Internet service for a campaign office of the candidate from a service provider that offers the service in the area where the office is located, the service provider shall provide the service:
(i) the request is made before the writ is issued, and
(ii) the writ is not issued within three days after the request; or
(b) within 48 hours after the writ is issued, in any other case.

Block.Para: 2. Subsection 495(4) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Para.change: (h.1) wilfully contravenes section 348.1 (failure to provide telephone, fax or Internet service in a timely manner);

Block.Para:
Published under authority of the Speaker of the House of Commons
